* Goal
      Make GitHub Copilot CLI usable inside Emacs on the current
      Ubuntu 24.04 container by enabling vterm end-to-end, with:

      1) Emacs built with dynamic-module support and a working
         libvterm/emacs-libvterm module available at runtime.
         Target builder:
         (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/emacs-30.1-treesitter-ubuntu-24.04/Dockerfile" 1)

      2) The devbox/container environment carrying the runtime pieces
         vterm needs (shared library path, shell env, install flow),
         adapted to the fact we are already inside a container and do
         not need the Azure/Arcadia tarball handoff.
         Targets:
         (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/devbox-ubuntu-24.04/Dockerfile" 1)
         (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/devbox-ubuntu-24.04/.bashrc" 1)
         (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/devbox-ubuntu-24.04/install.amd64.sh" 1)

      3) The current running Ubuntu 24 container updated directly so
         `M-x vterm` can be used now for ghcp CLI / Ink TUI work,
         without waiting for a host Docker rebuild/tar extraction step.

      Success criteria:
      - [X] In the current container, batch Emacs can `(require 'vterm)`
            without error.
            — verified: VTERM OK
      - [X] In the current container, `ldd` on `vterm-module.so`
            resolves libvterm correctly.
            — verified: libvterm.so.0 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libvterm.so.0
      - [ ] In interactive Emacs, `M-x vterm` opens a shell.
            — requires interactive terminal; module readiness verified.
      - [ ] Inside vterm, ghcp CLI can be launched for Ink/TUI use;
            `C-c C-t` still toggles `vterm-copy-mode`.
            — deferred to live TUI session.
      - [X] Ubuntu 24 builder files encode the same capability so the
            next rebuild reproduces the setup.

* Dependencies
      - Prior art / rationale:
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/.github/REPL/01.vterm.tui.org.txt" 1)
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/.github/REPL/fix.archive/emacs_ghcp/emacs.ghcpcli.md" 1)
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/.github/REPL/fix.archive/emacs_ghcp/fri_apr_24_2026_compatibility_of_vterm_with_ink_tui.md" 1)
      - Reference commit carrying the Azure Linux implementation:
            :b7bc77e:
      - Emacs-side wiring already present in repo:
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/bartuer-vterm.el" 1)
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/.emacs.el" 1437)
      - Upstream references:
            emacs-libvterm: https://github.com/akermu/emacs-libvterm
            libvterm: https://www.leonerd.org.uk/code/libvterm/
            Copilot CLI docs:
              https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/use-copilot-cli
      - Ubuntu 24 base image:
            ubuntu:24.04

* Context
      Azure/Arcadia already proved the product decision:
      - vterm is the correct terminal host for ghcp CLI / Ink TUI.
      - bartuer-vterm.el already sets the important runtime knobs:
            big scrollback, lower redraw delay, `C-y`, `M-y`,
            `C-c C-t`.
      - .emacs.el already loads bartuer-vterm opportunistically.

      What is missing on Ubuntu 24 today:
      - Emacs builder does NOT configure with `--with-modules`.
      - Emacs builder does NOT install libtool / libvterm headers.
      - No vendored `vendor/vterm` payload is built or shipped.
      - Current install.amd64.sh assumes tarball extraction only; for
        this task the active container should be provisioned directly.

      Ubuntu 24 adaptation rule:
      - Reuse the Azure logic, but translate it from "build tarballs,
        ship artifacts to another container" into "install/build in the
        current container, then make the builder files reproduce that
        exact state later".
      - No host docker orchestration is required in this iteration.

      Likely implementation shape:
      - apt install: cmake, libtool-bin, libvterm-dev (and any missing
        compiler/pkg-config prerequisites if required by vterm build).
      - ensure Emacs 30.1 configure includes `--with-modules`.
      - vendor/build emacs-libvterm into:
            /root/etc/el/vendor/vterm/
        so existing bartuer-vterm.el keeps working unchanged.
      - keep `LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/lib:/usr/lib...` style runtime
        visibility if the built module links to a non-default lib path.

      Non-goals:
      - Packaging a new tarball in this container-only pass.
      - Reworking bartuer-vterm.el unless Ubuntu-specific fallout
        proves it necessary.
      - Guaranteeing Ink redraw correctness without an interactive
        terminal check; module/build readiness comes first.

* DONE Capture Ubuntu 24 vterm baseline :test_tool:
      Goal: prove the current gap before editing.

      - [X] Check whether Emacs has module support now:
              ~/local/bin/emacs --batch \
                --eval '(princ system-configuration-features)'
              RESULT: no MODULES (pre-rebuild)

      - [X] Check whether any vterm payload already exists:
              ls -la /root/etc/el/vendor /root/etc/el/vendor/vterm
              RESULT: no vendor/vterm directory

      - [X] Check whether `libvterm` is already installed:
              dpkg -l | grep libvterm
              RESULT: libvterm-dev 0.3.3-2build1 already present

      - [X] Record the failure mode of:
              ~/local/bin/emacs --batch --eval "(require 'vterm)"
              RESULT: Cannot open load file: vterm (no MODULES, no payload)

* DONE Patch Ubuntu 24 emacs builder
      Goal: builder can reproduce a module-capable Emacs + vendored
      emacs-libvterm.

      - [X] Update
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/emacs-30.1-treesitter-ubuntu-24.04/Dockerfile" 1)
            to install the vterm build prerequisites:
              libtool-bin
              libvterm-dev

      - [X] Add `--with-modules` to the Emacs 30.1 configure line.

      - [X] Add vterm build/vendor step (same commit as Azure reference):
              ENV EMACS_LIBVTERM_COMMIT=54c29d14bca05bdd8ae60cda01715d727831e3f9
              builds against system libvterm, copies vterm.el + vterm-module.so
              into /root/etc/el/vendor/vterm/
              builder smoke-tests with batch (require 'vterm)

      - [X] Update amd64.tar.list to include libvterm .so paths and
              vendor/vterm directory so future tarball packaging ships vterm.

      - [X] Ubuntu 24 packaged libvterm (0.3.3) is sufficient;
            no source-from-scratch build needed.

* DONE Patch Ubuntu 24 devbox/runtime files
      Goal: runtime side matches the new vterm delivery model.

      - [X] Update
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/devbox-ubuntu-24.04/Dockerfile" 1)
            — no changes needed; `libvterm0` is already installed by apt as
            part of the tarball flow, and vterm-module.so is vendored.

      - [X] Update
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/devbox-ubuntu-24.04/.bashrc" 1)
            — no changes needed; PATH / LD_LIBRARY_PATH already correct.

      - [X] Update
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/devbox-ubuntu-24.04/install.amd64.sh" 1)
            added `ldconfig` after tarball extractions so the dynamic linker
            registers libvterm.so.
            No .bashrc or devbox Dockerfile changes were necessary.

* DONE Provision the active Ubuntu 24 container directly :test_tool:
      Goal: make vterm available now, without waiting for tarball flow.

      - [X] Install prerequisites directly with apt in the running
            container.
            apt-get install -y libtool-bin libvterm-dev
            — both already at newest version (libvterm-dev 0.3.3-2build1)

      - [X] Build/vendor emacs-libvterm directly into
            /root/etc/el/vendor/vterm/ so current .emacs.el picks it up.
            Cloned emacs-libvterm to /root/local/src/emacs-libvterm,
            checked out 54c29d14bca05bdd8ae60cda01715d727831e3f9.
            cmake -DUSE_SYSTEM_LIBVTERM=yes .. && make
            cp vterm.el vterm-module.so /root/etc/el/vendor/vterm/
            ldconfig

      - [X] Emacs binary lacked module support; rebuilt from source.
            Downloaded emacs-30.1.tar.gz to /root/local/src/.
            configure flags (adapted for live container):
              --prefix=/root/local
              --without-native-compilation   (libgccjit headers absent in live container)
              --with-gnutls=ifavailable       (libgnutls28-dev absent in live container)
              --with-modules
              --with-tree-sitter
              --without-x
            make -j$(nproc) && make install → /root/local/bin/emacs
            New features string: MODULES NOTIFY INOTIFY PDUMPER SECCOMP THREADS TREE_SITTER ZLIB

      - [X] Commands kept in plan so the container setup can be replayed
            outside this session.

* DONE Validate vterm readiness :test_tool:
      Main loop discipline:
      - Terminal A / shell 1: edit/build/install.
      - Terminal B / shell 2: run Emacs batch checks or interactive
        verification. Do not mix the test shell with the build shell.

      - [X] Batch require succeeds:
              ~/local/bin/emacs --batch \
                --eval "(add-to-list 'load-path \"/root/etc/el/vendor/vterm\")" \
                --eval "(require 'vterm)" \
                --eval "(message \"VTERM OK\")"
              RESULT: VTERM OK ✓

      - [X] Shared-library linkage looks sane:
              ldd /root/etc/el/vendor/vterm/vterm-module.so
              RESULT: libvterm.so.0 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libvterm.so.0 ✓

      - [ ] Interactive check:
              ~/local/bin/emacs -nw
              M-x vterm
              run a shell command
              press C-c C-t and confirm copy-mode toggles
              — requires interactive terminal session.

      - [ ] ghcp/Ink check:
              inside vterm run `copilot`
              verify redraw, arrows, ctrl keys, and resize are usable
              — deferred to live TUI session.

* HALT Main Loop :interrupt:
      Stop only when:
      - batch require passes,
      - interactive `M-x vterm` is usable in this container, and
      - Ubuntu 24 builder files capture the working setup.

      If the remaining blocker is only ghcp interactive verification,
      mark build/runtime work DONE and leave that last item explicitly
      open for a live TUI session.

* Cross-references
      - Azure reference implementation:
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/.github/REPL/01.vterm.tui.org.txt" 1)
      - Commit carrying Azure vterm work:
            :b7bc77e:
      - Emacs-side vterm glue:
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/bartuer-vterm.el" 1)
      - Ubuntu 24 targets:
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/emacs-30.1-treesitter-ubuntu-24.04/Dockerfile" 1)
            (link "/src/dot-emacs/build/devbox-ubuntu-24.04/Dockerfile" 1)
